summary: Dan Shapiro highlights a chisel review by David Wright that he considers the best he's ever read. The post serves as a brief recommendation, linking to Wright's PDF comparison of high-end chisels. Shapiro notes that Wright's review significantly influenced his own earlier writing about chisel shopping. Wright adds a postscript confirming his original opinions held up over extended use, with Two Cherries and Stanley 750 remaining his favorites.
key_points: Shapiro endorses David Wright's comprehensive high-end chisel review as exceptionally well-done
Wright's long-term use confirmed his original review findings, lending credibility to first-impression tool evaluations
The post reflects Shapiro's engagement with the woodworking community and hands-on maker culture
money_quotes: In fact, it was the article that had the greatest impact on me when I wrote this piece about chisel shopping.
Opinions formed when writing that article have generally been proven true over extended use as well.
The Sorby and Henry Taylor still dull quickly though it's easy to put a nice edge on them.
key_insight: A thoughtful initial tool review, when done rigorously, tends to hold up over years of real-world use.
